codeReview.summary: The fix keeps the public `InputItem` shape unchanged (`crates/agents-core/src/items.rs:10-12`) and the facade regression still proves downstream callers can construct and pattern-match `InputItem::Json { value }` without provenance fields (`crates/openai-agents/tests/openai_session_semantics.rs:115-179`). But the new commit does not actually make duplicate empty JSON objects identity-bearing: `json_value_identity` still returns `None` for `{}` because it only derives object identity from the first key (`crates/agents-core/src/internal/session_persistence.rs:215-218`). The follow-up change merely routes empty objects through the new-first frequency fallback (`crates/agents-core/src/internal/session_persistence.rs:233-235`), so a callback that returns the history-side `{}` is still indistinguishable from the new-turn `{}` and will now be persisted as fresh input. The added regression only covers returning the new-turn `{}` (`crates/agents-core/src/internal/session_persistence.rs:391-392`), so the prior blocking issue about empty objects lacking identity-bearing provenance is not fully resolved.

issues.description: `json_value_identity` still gives empty JSON objects no identity because the object branch only returns `Some(...)` when `map.iter().next()` exists (`crates/agents-core/src/internal/session_persistence.rs:215-218`). This fix therefore does not satisfy the requirement to treat duplicate `{}` values as identity-bearing during `session_input_callback` attribution. Instead it adds a new-first fallback for empty objects in `prefers_new_frequency_match` (`crates/agents-core/src/internal/session_persistence.rs:233-235`), which fixes the specific 'callback returned the new-turn `{}`' case but misclassifies the opposite case: if the callback returns the history-side `{}`, the item still has no reference identity and the new-first fallback will persist it as fresh input. The only new regression (`crates/agents-core/src/internal/session_persistence.rs:391-392`) covers the new-turn path, so this remaining history-vs-new ambiguity is still untested and still violates the expected behavior.

summary: Reviewed the fix feature transcript skeleton, both the original and follow-up session provenance diffs, the prior failed review, and the shared-state docs. The rerun preserves the public `InputItem` API, but it still fails scrutiny because duplicate empty JSON objects are not actually given internal identity, leaving history-side `{}` callback results misattributed as fresh input.
